def: s.f. Oneness, unity; soleness; solitariness; singularity, uniqueness, incomparableness, excellence; — union, conjunction; kinship; — unity, concord, unanimity.
s.f. = yagānagat, or yagānagī, q.v.
= H يگنوپويت yagnopavīt, s.m. corr. of yajṅopavīt, q.v.s.v. yajṅa.
s.m. Prey, plunder, booty, spoil, pillage, sacking, rapine.
s.m. Prey, plunder, booty, spoil, pillage, sacking, rapine.
s.m. Prey, plunder, booty, spoil, pillage, sacking, rapine.
s.m. corr. of يجن yajṅa or yagya, q.v.
= H يگوپويت yaggopavīt, s.m. corr. of yajṅopavīt, q.v.s.v. yajṅa.

ety: Ap. Prk. एहु or एह; S. ईदृशः
def: pron. (prox. demons.) This; this person; — he; she; it; — adv. In this, herein; here; — to the extent or degree (that), to this degree
subs: Here he is come, here he comes
See here, here it is (cf. wah, and wah dekho, &c.)
This is the condition; on condition (that), provided
conj. Neither this nor that; neither
(emphat.) This very one, the very same

def: adv. A dialec. form of yahāṅ, q.v.
adv. corr. of yahāṅ, q.v.

ety: (dialec.) ihāṅ; Ap. Prk. एहहं; S. ईदृशे; — or yah+ān = nāṅ = thān = Prk. थाणे or थाणि = S. स्थाने, loc. of स्थान
def: adv. In this place, here, hither; hereabout (yahāṅ is often incorrectly used as a postpn. in the sense of hāṅ ( = S. sthāne), 'at the abode' (of, -ke, or -mere, &c.), at; Fr. chez; Lat. apud)
subs: or yahāṅ-lag, or yahāṅ-lo or loṅ, adv. As far as this, to this place, thus far; — to this degree or pitch; to such an extent, so much so (that), insomuch (that); — until; — up to this time, hitherto
adj. (f. -kī), Of or belonging to this place
adv. In this very place; exactly here

r: yahī, yahi, yěhī, yahai, yah, ī, hī
def: pron. This very, this same, the very same (person or thing); — this even.
pron. emphat. = yahī, q.v.
adv. In this very place, just here
subs: adv. From this very place; — on this very account; hence.

ety: Ap. Prk. एम्वहिं or एव; S. इयत् (Vedic ईवत्)
def: adv. (old H.) Now; at the present time (syn. ab; is samay).
s.m. A Jew, a Hebrew.
prop. n. Judah (Jacob's fourth son); Judas; Jude.
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to the Jews; Hebrew, Jewish; Judæan; Judaical; — a Jew, a Hebrew.

ety: fem. of yahūdī; — inī = S. इनी
def: s.f. A Jewess.
adj. = yahūdī, q.v.
(for A. يہودية yahūdīyat), prop. n. Judea.
s.m. (f. -ikā), A sacrificing priest, &c. (see the pop. form. jājak); — a royal elephant; a furious elephant.
s.m. See the pop. form جاجك jājak.
s.f. = S ياجکتو याजकत्व yājakatva, s.m. The condition, or the office, of a sacrificing priest; priestship, priesthood.
s.m. = S ياجکتا याजकता yājakatā, s.f. The condition, or the office, of a sacrificing priest; priestship, priesthood.
adj. Of or relating to a priest or to the priestly office; priestly, sacerdotal.
s.m. See the pop. form ججمان jajmān.
s.f. See the pop. form jajmānī.
s.m. See the pop. form jājan.
adj. Belonging to sacrifice.
s.m. corr. of yajna or yagya, q.v.
s.m. A sacrifice, an offering, oblation; a ceremony in which oblations are offered (i.q. jaj; jag; the pop. forms)
subs: or yagya, s.m. A form of Vishṇu; Vishṇu in the Boar-incarnation
s.f. 'Sacrifice-ground,' a place prepared for a sacrifice, or a place where a sacrifice is offered
s.m. An animal for sacrifice, a victim; a horse
s.m. A species of fig-tree, Ficus glomerata
s.m. A place for sacrifice; an altar
s.f. The completion or accomplishment of a sacrifice; the due performance of a sacrificial ceremony; — obtaining the objects of a sacrifice

s.m. A sacred cord worn over the left shoulder and under the right arm at sacrifices; the sacrificial thread or cord (originally worn by the Brāhmaṇ, the Kshatriya, and the Vaiśya, as distinctive of their castes, but now confined to the Brahmanical order)
or sālā, s.f. A sacrificial hall; a house or place for keeping the sacrificial fire; a temple; shrine
s.m. A sacrificial act, a sacrificial rite or ceremony
s.m. Name of a Rākshas (mentioned in the Rāmāyaṇ)
s.m. One who is invested with the sacrificial thread
s.f. An altar for sacrifice; a place prepared for a sacrifice.

def: adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to sacrifice; sacrificial; — a sacrificer, the officiating priest at a sacrificial ceremony; the institutor of a sacrifice; one skilled in sacrificial rites.
adj. (f. -ā), Belonging to sacrifices, sacrificial; — fit or suitable for sacrifice.
adj. Worthy of worship or sacrifice; sacred; — belonging to worship; relating to sacrifice; suitable or fit for sacrifice; sacrificial
subs: s.m. 'Sacrificial country'; the country of the Hindūs, Hindūstān, or that region in which sacrificial ceremonies can be duly performed, or the country in which the black antelope is native.

def: s.m. See s.v. yajṅa or yagya.
s.m. Gog and Magog; — (fig.) a pair of bad characters.

ety: changeable, in comp., to यजु yajuh̥, and यजुऱ yajur
def: s.m. A sacrificial prayer or formula; a text of the Yajur-veda, or a technical term for certain words or mantras (not restricted to metre) muttered in a peculiar manner at a sacrifice
subs: s.m. 'The sacrificial Veda,' the second of the four Vedas (it is divided into two portions, the Black and the White, both of which are very full on the subject of religious rites).

def: adj. & s.m. See the pop. form jājya.

ety: Pehl. evak; old P. aiva; Zend aêva; S. एक
def: adj. One; a, an
subs: adv. One by one; — all at once, suddenly, immediately; — one opposed to another
or yak-bāragī, vulg. yak-bārgī, or yak-bāra, or yak-bārī, s.f. & adv. One time or turn; — once; — at once, all at once, immediately, suddenly
adv. One by one; successively; — suddenly, all at once
adj. Striving always to go on one side, pressing on one rein (a horse)
s.m. One piece; one part or segment; — adj. & adv. All of a piece; solid, massive; — at one stroke, at once
s.m. A guitar with one wire or string
s.m. One place; — adv. In one place, together
adj. Descended from a common ancestor, of the same stock; lineal, direct (syn. ham-jaddī)
adj. & s.m. Of one accord, unanimous; — determined, resolved; — single-minded, or single-hearted, true, sincere; — a friend
vulg. yak-jihtī, s.f. Unanimity; concord; friendship
adj. One-eyed; — yak-ćashmī (or yak-ruḵẖī) taṣwīr, s.f. A profile
adj. & adv. Some little, somewhat, a little; — some little time, sometime
adv. A little time, a while
adj. Entire; uniform, even (cloth); — homogeneous; — what can be lifted with one hand; — adv. Altogether
adj. One-handed; — s.f. Name of a trick in wrestling
or yak-dīgar, pron. & adv. One another; each other; — mutually
adj. Of one heart or mind, unanimous; single-hearted, true, loyal, sincere; — adv. Unanimously, with one accord
adj. Of the same tribe, sect, or caste; consubstantial
adj. Going on the same road or journey
adj. One-sided; — of the same appearance on both sides; — s.f. A kind of bow
or yak-ranga, adj. Of one colour; — invariable, uniform; — congenial; — simple; sincere, faithful, true
s.f. Uniformity; — congeniality; — simplicity; sincerity; friendship
adj. Of one face; — written on one side; — unanimous; — sincere, pure, loyal
adj. Of one day; for a day, ephemeral
s.f. Unanimity; friendship; simplicity, sincerity, singleness of heart
adv. In one way; by one road; once; — at once; at one glance, at first sight
adj. Of one voice or tongue; unanimous; — uniform of opinion or speech, free from duplicity

or yak-sāṅ, or yak-sar, adj. & adv. The same; equal; like, similar; matching; uniform; conformable; regular; — plain, level; parallel; — of equal sides; of the same order; — the same; ditto; in the same manner, equally; in the same series or order
adj. Of one year; one year old, yearling; — for one year, lasting one year
vulg. yak-sāṅ, adj. & adv. See yak-sār
or yak-sāniyat, s.f. Uniformity, identity, sameness, parity, &c. (see yak-sān)
adv. Under one head; — in one body, all together; at one stroke, all at once, suddenly; — from beginning to end; — alone
adj. & adv. Even, uniform; equal; fixed, settled; — on one side, aside; together; — alike
adj. Even-tempered; of a mild disposition
adj. Of one shot, single-barrelled (gun or pistol)
adv. On one side, aside, apart, separate
adj. One-sided; by one side or party (in a law-suit); ex parte
or yak-faslī, adj. Producing only one crop annually (land)
adj. & adv. Consistent (writer); — all, total; — together; entirely; at one stroke, at once
adj. Of a single fold, or breadth, &c.; single; — s.f. A veil, or cloak, or sheet (worn over the shoulders) of one breadth and without a seam
adj. & adv. Of one piece or leather (as a shoe, &c.); — all one piece; all at once, altogether, completely, entirely
(better ek-lohī), s.f. A sword-blade made of one piece of steel
adj. Of one or the same mother
adv. One time, once; — at once, at one blow
s.m. A handful; — prompt payment; — adv. All at once
adj. & s.m. Of one and the same native place; — compatriot; countryman
adj. Of one and the same time

def: adj. A dialec. (and colloq.) form = ايك ek, q.v.
adj. One, single, sole, solitary; singular, unique, unrivalled, incomparable (i.q. yakkā, q.v.); — adv. At once, all at once; together.
s.f. = اکائي ikāʼī, q.v.
adj. & adv. (Braj) = akelā, q.v.
s.m. The letter or sound ya.
s.m. (dialec.) = ekatrā = ekotrā, q.v.
adj. & adv. (dialec.) = akelā, q.v.
s.m. Ice
adj. 'Cooked, dressed, prepared'), s.f. Gravy (prepared to dress pulāʼo in); sauce; stew, hash; a rich stew of meat
subs: s.m. A kind of pulāʼo prepared with gravy, or boiled in broth.

ety: v.n. of the measure faʻīl, fr. يَقِنَ 'to know for certain'
def: s.m. Certain of sure knowledge, certainty, assurance, confidence, conviction, belief, opinion; truth, true faith, infallibility, evidence; — adj. Certain, sure, positive, true; — adv. For certain, with certainty, truly
subs: (-ko), The certainty or assurance to possess (one), to be or become assured (of, -kā), to believe
v.t. To know for certain, to be assured or positive (of)
or yaqīn karānā (-ko), To cause (one) to believe; to assure
(-par), To pin (one's) faith (to), to believe, to credit
v.t. To regard as certain or true, to be assured of; to believe
or yaqīn-karo, or yaqīn jāno, Be assured; believe (me)
v.t. Not to believe; to mistrust

def: adv. Certainly, surely, assuredly, truly, infallibly.
s.f. Certainty; infallibility; truth (i.q. yaqīn, q.v.).
adj. Certain, sure, indisputable, infallible, real, positive; scientific; belonging to truth or faith.
(pl. of yaqīnī, or rather of yaqinīyat, fem. of yaqīnī), s.f. pl. Things certain, certainties, truths (as articles of religion, demonstrations of geometry, &c.).
adj. & s.m. = يکا yakkā, q.v.

ety: i.q. ekā; ikkā; and P. yaka, yakka
def: adj. One, single, solitary; sole, unique, &c. (i.q. ikkā; yaka); — s.m. The ace (at cards, &c.; i.q. ekā); — a one-horse chaise (i.q. P. yakka); a kind of one-horse vehicle peculiar to India (i.q. ikkā); — a lamp with a place for one wick ( = ikkā); — a trooper who serves alone ( = ikkā)
subs: adj. & s.m. Attacking (the foe) singly; — the phœnix or hero (of the age)

def: adj. (dialec.) = ek-bhī, 'one even.'
s.m. (dialec.) = ekotrā, q.v.
adj. See s.v. yak (and cf. ek-sā, or ek-sāṅ, s.v. ek).
s.m. A kind of demi-god attendant on Kuver, and employed in the care of his garden and treasures (see the pop. forms jakh, and jak); — a name of Kuver; — a name of the palace of Indra
subs: s.m. King of the Yakshas; — a name of Kuver

def: s.f. A female Yaksh; — name of Kuver's wife; — a sort of female fiend, attached to the service of Durgā, and frequently, like a sylph or fairy, maintaining intercourse with mortals.
s.f. = yakshī, q.v.
s.m. = yakshmā, q.v.
s.m. Pulmonary disease, consumption, decline.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Consumptive, phthisical; — one who suffers from pulmonary consumption.

r: yaktā, yak, tā, tāk, tāh, tah
ety: yak, 'one,' q.v.+suff. tā = Pehl. tāk, and i.q. tāh or tah, qq.v.
def: adj. Single; simple; — singular, unique, incomparable; — s.m. A garment without a lining.
s.f. Singleness; unity; — singularity, the being unique, incomparableness.
adj. A dialec. form of ايك ek, q.v.
(fr. عقب 'to strike the heel'; — to come close after; to follow'), prop. n. Jacob; James.

ety: yak, q.v.+um = Zend ma = S. म
def: adj. First; — the first of the month.
s.m. A ruby; — a garnet.
adj. Of or relating to the ruby; ruby-; — s.f. Name of an electuary.
s.f. Mane (of a horse; — orig. 'neck of a horse').

ety: by aphæresis, fr. Zend vīra; S. वीर
def: s.m. A hero, a brave man, warrior, champion, an athlete
subs: or yal-shal, adj. Corpulent, stout, strong, robust.

def: s.m. Deliverance, escape, release, liberation; — adj. Free, set free or at liberty; turned loose, allowed to roam or pasture at liberty; — sole, single, solitary; — crooked; — absurd, vain, false, foolish, trifling; — s.f. A bad woman, a harlot.
adj. Unconfined; extended, spread out, stretched out; — adv. At ease.
s.m.(?) The longest night of the year, or the first night of winter (in or near which the sun enters Capricorn).
s.m. = الغار ilg̠ār, q.v.
s.m. = الغار ilg̠ār, q.v.

ety: corr. of aiyām, pl. of yaum
def: s.m. Season, time; weather.

s.m. (rt. yā), Going, proceeding, marching; motion; course; — a night-watch, a period or watch of three hours; — (rt. yam) restraint, forbearance; — cessation, termination; — adj. Of or relating to Yama; coming or derived from Yama; done or ordered by Yama.

s.m. Restraining, controlling, subduing, taming, keeping in check; restraint; — self-control; penance; any great moral duty or observance (as opp. to niyam); — a twin, one of a pair or couple, a fellow; — a symbolical expression for the number 2; — the regent of the infernal regions, &c. (see the pop. form jam); — death; time
subs: s.m., or yam-purī, s.f. The city or abode of Yam; the infernal regions
vulg. yamaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -jā), Twin-born; — a twin; one of a pair or brace
vulg. yam-dutiyā, s.m. Yam's messenger or minister (employed to bring the souls of the dead to Yam's judgment-seat, and thence conduct them to their final destination; — see jam-dūt)
or yam-dutiyā, s.m. See the pop. form jam-dutiyā
s.m. A kind of dagger or poniard (see jam-dhar)
s.m. The time of death; — an epithet of Yam
s.m. Yam's world, the region of Yam.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. yama or yam.
s.m. See the pop. form جمك jamak
s.f. A kind of hiccough; — name of a Tantra deity; — name of a river.
s.m. A pair, couple, brace (i.q. jamal)
subs: s.m. Two Arjun-trees which obstructed the path of Krishṇ when a child and were uprooted by him.

def: s.m. See s.v. yama or yam.
s.f. A pair; — a sort of dress consisting of two pieces (body and petticoat).
s.m. A division of the Rāg or musical mode called Kalyāṇ.
s.m. A Yavan; an Ionian, a Greek, &c. (see yavan, and jawan).
s.m. The country of Yemen, Arabia Felix.
s.f. The plant Ptychotis ajowan (see yawānī).
s.f. The wife of Yam, &c. (i.q. Yamī).
adj. = يمنی yamanī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = jāmnī, q.v.
adj. Of or belonging to Yemen or Arabia Felix; — s.m. A cornelian.
s.f. See the pop. form jāmnī
subs: s.f. The language or dialect of the Yavans; Persian; Arabic.

def: s.m. = جاماتا jāmātā, q.v.
s.m. = جاماتا jāmātā, q.v.
s.m. = جاماتا jāmātā, q.v.
s.f. Name of Yam's twin-sister (identified in post-Vedic mythology with the river goddess Yamunā); name of the wife of Yam; — a twin-sister.
s.f. See the pop. form jāmī.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), See the pop. form jāmik.
s.f. Night (i.q. yāminī).
s.f. A twin-sister.
s.m. An oath; — the right hand, or side, or part; right wing (of an army)
subs: s.m. Right and left; — the two wings (of an army).

def: s.f. Night (i.q. jāminī, q.v.).
s.m. The ocean, sea; — a great river (syn. daryā).
s.f. (dialec. or corr.) = jamhāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Antimony; sulphuret of antimony, collyrium.
s.m. (dialec. or corr.) = jamun, q.v.
s.f. corr. of next, q.v.
s.f. The river Jamnā (see jamunā); — (in Myth.) the twin-sister of Yama and daughter of Sūrya
subs: s.m. Bank (or banks) of the Jamnā
vulg. jamnottrī (˚nā+ut-tara+ikā), s.f. The place where the Jamnā issues from the (Himālaya) mountains, the source of the Jamnā.

ety: prob. fr. S. यावनं, rt. यु 'to bind; to join'
def: s.m. (hill dialect) Snow, ice (Bate)
pron. (dialec. Rām.) = jis-ko.
adv. = yahāṅ-se, q.v.; — 2˚ pron. = is-se, q.v.
adj. (dialec., f. -ī), Ignorant, unwise; — young; childish (opp. to syānā)
subs: To continue (or be) ignorant (as a child); — to be young, to be under age.

def: conj. (dialec. or colloq.) = yā-nā, or yā-nahīṅ, 'or not.'

s.m. Going, moving, proceeding; riding; marching; marching (against an enemy), attacking; — a conveyance or vehicle of any kind, a carriage, chariot, waggon, car; a litter, palanquin; a horse, an elephant, &c.
conj. (dialec. or colloq.) = yā nahīṅ, 'or not.'
(dialec. or colloq.) = yah-nām, 'this name.'
adv. A corr. of يعني yaʻnī, q.v.

ety: 3rd pers. sing. of the aorist of عَنَی 'he meant, or intended'
def: adv. That is to say, viz., i.e., to wit, videlicet; — for, because.
s.m. See the pop. form jantra or jantar
subs: (˚tra+āl˚), s.m. Any establishment where machines are made, sold, or worked; an engine-house; a printing-office, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Restraining, checking, controlling, binding; — a restrainer, tamer, subduer; — pain, anguish.
s.f. Restraint, control; constraint, compulsion, force; — affliction, pain, anguish, torture, torment.

s.m. (S. यन्त्र+इन्+कः), Acquainted (or having to do) with instruments or machinery, mechanical; — ingenious; — crafty, designing; — a maker of instruments or machines; a machinist; mechanician; artisan.
s.f. Any instrument or means for binding; a chain, fetters; — (in Med.) a bandage.
part. (f. -ā), Restrained, curbed, checked; bound, fastened, fettered, confined.
s.m. Causing to go; driving away, expelling; expulsion, ejection; removal; rejection; — causing (time) to pass away, spending or passing away time; wasting time; — delay, procrastination.
suff. (another form of wār, q.v.), Lord, possessor (used to form possessive adjectives, e.g. shahr-yār, q.v.).
adj. Wanton; — s.f. A wanton woman, a harlot
subs: adj. & s.m. Friendly, cordial; merry, jolly; voluptuous, sensual; — a good fellow, a jolly fellow (i.q. yār-bāsh ādmī)
(with gen.), To become the friend (of); to be friends (with), be intimate (with); — yār banānā (-ko), To make a friend of; to form a friendship with
vulg. yār-gār, s.m. 'A companion in a cave'; an intimate friend; — a name of Abū-bakr (who continued the companion and friend of Muhammad when the latter fled for refuge to a cave)
To make friends; to make a friend of (-ko); to take (to oneself) a lover or paramour
s.m. One who deceives or cheats his friend or friends, a deceiver, a cheat; one who betrays his friend, a traitor
s.f. Deceiving, or cheating, or betraying a friend
s.m. A faithful or sincere friend; a constant lover
(-kā, or merā, &c.), To contract friendship (with), to be friendly (with).

ety: v.n. fr. yārastan = ārastan, 'to be able,' &c.; rt. Zend ā+rād = S. आ+राध (रध्नोति)
def: s.m. Strength, force, power (of resistance); — boldness, courage (see yāra).
s.m. Friendship; love (more com. yārī, q.v.).
s.m. A broad bracelet; — a collar (for the neck); — the wrist; — a glove, gauntlet; — a wound; — power, strength (i.q. yārā); — a bold man; a shrewd man; — necessity; — tax, toll, revenue
s.m. Arms, accoutrements, weapons; apparatus; a dart.

ety: yār, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Friendly; — in a friendly manner; — s.m. Friendliness
subs: adv. In friendliness, in a friendly way.

def: s.m. = yarg̠ā, q.v.
s.m. An amble (pace of a horse); — name of a bird; — a kind of fancy embroidery or rich needlework
s.m. Hostage, security, bail.
s.f. Friendship, intimacy; love; — companionship; — favour; — assistance, aid; — intriguing, intrigue; — share, portion
subs: (-se), To form a friendship (with); — to form a liaison (with)
(-ko), or yārī karnā (-kī), To assist, help, favour, benefit, serve, do good offices (to), befriend; — to second, abet, side (with)
(-se), To be estranged (from); to be cuts (with).

def: s.m. The yellow jaundice.
s.f. A female friend; a inistress.

ety: prop. yaʼs, inf. n. of يأس 'to despair,' &c.
def: s.f. Despair, desperation, hopelessness, despondency; — fear, terror
subs: (-ko), To be filled with despair; to be despondent.

def: adj. See s.v. yaś.
s.m. Glory, splendour, lustre; fame, renown, distinction, celebrity, name, reputation, character; praise, eulogium; — luck (see the pop. form jas)
subs: s.m. Good and bad reputation, fame and infamy; praise and dispraise
or yaś-pat, or jas-pat, adj. & s.m. (f. -patnī), Famous, excellent, renowned, reputable; — a famous man, a celebrity
s.m. An encomiast, a panegyrist, eulogist
adj. & s.m. Honour-loving; desirous or eager for fame; ambitious; — one who is eager for fame, an ambitious man
adj. Causing renown, conferring fame or distinction, rendering famous or glorious; famous, glorious

r: yāsamin, yāsmin, yāsaman, yāsimīn, yāsmīn, yāsamūn, yāsamīn
def: s.m. Jasmine
subs: (or -bū), Scented like jasmine; redolent of jasmine.

def: s.m. Plenty, affluence, opulence; — ease; — adj. & s.m. Left (hand, &c.); — the left side; — an unlucky person

ety: changeable in comp. to yaśah̥, or yaśo
def: s.m. Glory, splendour; fame, &c. (see the contrac. form yaś)

r: ye, yā, yāʼě-ḥut̤t̤ī, ḥut̤t̤ī, yāʼě-mus̤annāt-ě-taḥtānī, ya, y, youth., alif, wāw, ḥurūf-ě-ʻillat, ī, bīn, yāʼě-maʻrūf, e, peṛ, yāʼě-majhūl, yā.
def: (called yāʼě-ḥut̤t̤ī, 'the yā of the word ḥut̤t̤ī,' or yāʼě-mus̤annāt-ě-taḥtānī, 'the yā with two dots below') is the thirty-fifth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the thirty-second of the Persian, and the twenty-eighth of the Arabic alphabet). It corresponds, as a consonant, to the Devanāgarī य ya, and has the sound of the English y in youth. Like alif and wāw, this letter is also one of the ḥurūf-ě-ʻillat, or 'letters of weakness,' being sometimes used as a vowel, as has been shown at the beginning of the first letter in this volume. In this case, when it helps to produce the vowel sound ī (as in بِين bīn), it is called yāʼě-maʻrūf, 'the known yā'; and when it forms the diphthong e (as in پيڙ peṛ), it is called yāʼě-majhūl, 'the unknown yā.' When used as a number, ي stands for 10; and in almanacs it is the character for Jupiter and the sign Aquarius.
pron. (nom. pl. of yah, q.v.), These; — they

ety: yudhi, loc. of yudh, 'battle' + sthira, 'firm or steady'
def: s.m. Name of the eldest of the five reputed sons of Pāṇḍu (he was the leader of the great war, related in the Mahā-bhārat, between the Pāṇḍavs and the Kurus, in the beginning of the Kali-yug).
s.m. A yoke; — a pair, brace, couple; — a term used in the game of ćausar (the opp. of a blot, two or more men standing on the same square); — an age, a period, &c. (see the pop. form jug).
s.m. See the pop. form جگل jugal.
(युग+अन्त), s.m. = S يگانتر युगान्तर yugāntar (युग+अन्तर), s.m. & adv. See the pop. forms jugānt, and jugāntar, s.v. jug.
(युग+अन्तर), s.m. & adv. = S يگانت युगान्त yugānt (युग+अन्त), s.m. See the pop. forms jugānt, and jugāntar, s.v. jug.
s.m. See the pop. form jugam.
s.m. = yūh = yūth, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of يوتهہ yūth, q.v.
s.m. = S يوکا यूका yūkā, s.f. A louse (see jūṅ).
